summary: Finish the activation→registration rename: align the retired world-edge vocabulary to the corpus noun across SDK emit, the lock column, engine types, and finding strings

edit.description: The SDK is mid-rename: `kind.ts`/`builtins.ts` already carry the `Registration` type and the `registration` KindFacts field, but this file still emits the lock column as `activation` and names the retired vocabulary. Rename the IR field `activation?` → `registration?`, `activationLabel` → `registrationLabel`, and the emitted declaration-row key `activation:` → `registration:`. This is the lock's on-disk column name — it MUST change in lockstep with src/builtin_lock.toml and src/drift.rs or tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs (re-derives the built-in lock from this module and byte-compares) breaks.

The embedded built-in lock. Rename the kind-row key `activation` → `registration` in all three rows (memory/rule/skill). Must match the SDK's emitted key (declarations.ts) exactly — this file is byte-compared against the SDK re-derivation.

KindFactRow: rename field `activation: Option<String>` → `registration`, and the two lock-column sites — `table.insert("activation", …)` on serialize and `str_col(table, "activation")` on parse — to `"registration"`. Also cut the altitude-vocab comment residue in this file (the `frozen` doc-comment's "altitude's package-fetch step", ladder-vocab retirement) per the rust.md exit clause.

Rename the `Activation` enum → `Registration` (variants Always/PathsMatch/DescriptionTrigger/Event unchanged), the `CustomKind.activation` field → `registration`, `activation_from_label` → `registration_from_label`, `KindFactRow::activation` reads, and the `#[cfg(test)]` sites. Reduce the dead section-title cites (`"Activation — a kind's inherent world-edges"` — now `"Registration — the kind's world fact"`) to file-path pointer tags per the rust.md exit clause.
Rename the imported `Activation` → `Registration` and the three `Activation::{DescriptionTrigger,PathsMatch,Always}` constructors in the built-in kind definitions.

Rename the `Activation` type, the `activations: &BTreeMap<&str, …>` params/locals, `dead_activation` → `dead_registration`, `live_members`/`reachability_orphaned` internals, and the user-facing finding string "the activation edge from the {} node … is dead" → "registration edge" (no-synonyms, specs/architecture/50-distribution.md). Update the in-file `#[cfg(test)]` assertions on that text.

Rename the `Activation` import, the `activations` params threaded through `why`/`explain`, and the orphan finding text ("its own activation is dead" → "registration") plus the in-file test assertions on it. Also cut the package-noun comment residue here ("the package its kind binds", ~296) and the altitude-vocab comment (~104) per the exit clause.

Rename the `activations` BTreeMap built in the dispatch and passed to check/read (~465-488). Cut the package-noun comment residue in this file: the dead cite `"Artifact kinds & package binding"` (~572; no such heading — reduce to a file-path pointer tag), "the prior by-package binding list" (~63), "built-in packages embedded" (~175), "dispatched to its floor package" (~575), and the altitude comments (~478 "until an altitude serializes mentions", ~803 "carriage locus") per the exit clause.
Update the ~31 `Activation`/`activations`/finding-text references to the renamed symbols and strings.
Update the `activations`/finding-text references to the renamed symbols and strings.
Update the ~7 references asserting the lock's `activation` column to the renamed `registration` column.
Update the two `activation` lock-column references to `registration`.

tests.asserts: The built-in lock re-derived from @dtmd/temper/claude-code still byte-matches src/builtin_lock.toml after the column is renamed to `registration` on both sides — the lockstep proof (no edit needed here; it names no column, but it fails loud if SDK emit and the embedded toml disagree).
Kind rows carry a `registration` label (`always`, `paths-match(paths)`, `description-trigger(description)`), not `activation`.
A dead world-edge produces a finding worded "registration edge … is dead"; reachability closure over registration edges is unchanged behaviorally.

acceptance: No `activation`/`Activation` symbol or prose survives in src/ or sdk/src/ (grep clean except legitimate `active` enum values and reporter.rs's `carriage return`); the lock column is `registration` end to end; findings speak "registration edge"; `cargo test` green including the frozen re-derivation.
notes: Atomic rename — cannot be split (a half-renamed enum won't compile). Sole owner of its files; nothing runs parallel over them. The one hazard is the lock-column lockstep: sdk/src/declarations.ts (emit) + src/builtin_lock.toml (embedded) + src/drift.rs (read/write) must change together or builtin_lock_frozen fails. Finding-string changes are gate-relevant (no-synonyms, 50-distribution), not cosmetic.

summary: Cut the package-noun and altitude comment residue plus dead section-title cites in the three files the first sweep and the rename entry do not touch (contract.rs, bundle.rs, extract.rs)

per.section: Decision: clause arrays and two quantifiers, not a package noun

edit.description: Cut the package-noun narration and dead section-title cites on `Contract`/`Clause`: `guidance` doc quoting `"Packages"` (~32, no such heading) and "a package that authors none" (~36); `Clause::guidance` quoting `"Templates"` (~52, no such heading); `Clause::source` quoting `"Decision: a built-in package is named for its source…"` (~59, no such heading — the real Decision is the package-noun one this entry cites). Reduce each to a bare file-path pointer tag (rust.md exit clause) or cut where the surrounding sentence already carries the intent.

Cut the package-noun comment residue: "taste lives in the packages, never here" (~52 — the concept is floors, not packages) and the PLUGIN_DESCRIPTION prose "with the std-lib packages embedded" (~55). The description string is user-facing plugin metadata — align it to the corpus noun (the built-in floors / the default program), not "packages".

Cut the altitude-vocab comment residue: "interpolation stays an altitude feature until a…" (~207; the ladder vocabulary — rungs/floors/altitude — was retired, specs/process/90-spec-system.md). Reduce to the plain deferral statement with a file-path pointer tag.

acceptance: grep of contract.rs/bundle.rs/extract.rs for `package`/`Packages`/`altitude` and the dead section titles ("Packages", "Templates", "a built-in package is named") is clean (except the legitimate `SDK_PACKAGE`-style npm-package identifiers, which are real); `cargo test` unchanged (comments only, plus one plugin-description string).
notes: Comments-only plus one user-facing plugin-description string. Disjoint from RENAME-ACTIVATION-TO-REGISTRATION (that entry owns read/main/drift/graph and folds their package+altitude comments in via the exit clause; this owns the three files it does not touch). reporter.rs's "carriage return" is a real CR char, left alone.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
